claim: BTC is still more likely than not to resolve Yes, but the best post-synthesis view is below the market: Bitcoin being around 74.2k with less than a day left supports a high-Yes base case, yet a single Binance 1-minute noon-ET close above 72k is narrow enough that 93.5% still looks somewhat overconfident.

frontmatter.contract_ambiguity_reason: minor residual UI/API candle-mapping uncertainty for the exact Binance noon-ET minute
independently_verified_points: Polymarket rules specify Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute final Close as source of truth
Threshold is strictly greater than 72000, not greater-than-or-equal
Upstream direct Binance checks placed BTCUSDT around 74.2k on Apr 15, materially above strike
ET-to-UTC mapping for Apr 16 noon ET corresponds to 16:00 UTC under EDT
frontmatter.verification_gap_summary: No fresh independent volatility/event check materially tightened overnight downside risk beyond the upstream Binance/rules verification.
frontmatter.best_countercase_summary: A routine-for-BTC 3% selloff or exact-minute wick before settlement could still flip this to No despite the current cushion.
frontmatter.main_reason_for_disagreement: Personas mainly differed on how much to discount the market for exact-minute path risk versus current spot cushion.
frontmatter.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes resolves only if Binance BTC/USDT's Apr 16 12:00 ET 1-minute candle final Close is strictly above 72000.
frontmatter.freshness_driver: BTC spot level and realized volatility into the Apr 16 noon ET settlement minute
decision_blockers: Residual overnight/morning BTC volatility can still erase the ~3% cushion
No stronger independent verification of tail-risk magnitude beyond lane-level exchange/rules checks
Minor exact-candle operational interpretation risk remains until live settlement bar is observed
frontmatter.next_checkpoint: Fresh Binance BTC/USDT check near Apr 16 11:55-12:01 ET.

sections.Alpha summary: Market implied probability is 0.935; my syndicated range is 0.87-0.90. That is a modest below-market view, actionable only as a small calibration edge rather than a large alpha call. The likely market mispricing is mild overconfidence in a one-minute crypto threshold settle given remaining path risk.
sections.Input coverage: All five personas were available and usable: base-rate, catalyst-hunter, market-implied, risk-manager, and variant-view. Supporting assumption/evidence artifacts were selectively used, especially the market-implied evidence map and Binance/Polymarket source notes. Coverage is complete because no persona is missing and the lane conclusions were internally consistent enough to synthesize, though sourcing independence is still only moderate.
sections.Market-implied baseline: The baseline is the quoted market probability of 0.935 Yes at snapshot time 2026-04-15T18:06:44Z. The swarm baseline sat materially below that at roughly 0.86-0.89, centered near 0.89.
sections.Syndicated probability estimate: My final post-synthesis estimate is 0.87 to 0.90 Yes. That keeps Yes as the clear base case because BTC was verified by the lanes around 74.2k, but it preserves nontrivial downside/timing risk for a sub-24h single-minute settlement market.
sections.Difference from swarm-implied center: This is not materially different from the swarm-implied center of 0.89. I stayed close to it because the sidecars were broadly faithful to the raw findings and the available verification supported the main mechanism: clean rules, correct venue, current price above strike. I did not move further away from market because no fresh synthesis-stage evidence showed the swarm was too bearish, but I also did not compress toward market because verification did not justify trusting 0.935.
sections.Agreement or disagreement with market: I disagree modestly with the market. The market is directionally right that Yes is favored, but it appears a bit too close to certainty for a contract that can fail on one bad minute after only about a 3% drawdown from the checked level.
sections.Independent verification of edge: Independent verification was medium quality, not high. What was checked well upstream and accepted in synthesis: contract wording, exact strict-greater-than condition, Binance BTC/USDT as source of truth, ET-to-UTC timing mechanics, and direct Binance price context around 74.2k. What remained weaker: no strong independent quantification of overnight downside odds, and web-search-based fresh external context was unavailable in synthesis because search hit bot-detection. That is enough to support a modest below-market view, but not enough for a large anti-market call.
sections.Compression toward market due to verific: No. I did not materially compress toward the market because the swarm-vs-market gap was only moderate and the existing verification did support the swarm's core skepticism: exact-minute path risk is real, and current cushion is comfortable rather than decisive. I also did not widen into a more aggressive below-market stance because verification quality was not strong enough for that.
sections.Timing and catalyst posture: The key checkpoint is the Binance BTC/USDT path into the Apr 16 noon ET settle minute. Edge decay is likely if BTC remains comfortably above 74k into late morning ET, because the market's high Yes price would then look increasingly justified. Waiting can improve the decision only if a fresh near-settlement check is feasible; otherwise the current edge is mostly a fragile timing-calibration view.
sections.Decision blockers: Main blockers are residual BTC volatility over the remaining window, the narrow one-minute settlement mechanic, and only medium-quality independent verification of tail-risk magnitude. These are caution flags more than reasons to halt a decision.
sections.Implication for the question: The best current interpretation is still Yes, but not at near-lock confidence. A downstream decision-maker should treat this as high-probability but not complacency-safe.
sections.Consensus across personas: All personas agreed on the core contract mechanics: Binance BTC/USDT, Apr 16 noon ET, 1-minute candle final Close, strictly above 72,000. All agreed BTC was materially above the strike at review time around 74.2k and that Yes is the directional base case. All also agreed the market is somewhat too confident because a single-minute crypto settle preserves meaningful path risk.
sections.Key disagreements across personas: The main disagreement was timing/weighting based rather than factual. Variant-view placed more weight on exact-minute path dependence and landed at 0.86; the others clustered at 0.89 and gave more credit to current cushion and absence of identified bearish catalysts. There was also minor source-of-truth caution around Binance UI/API candle labeling, but no lane argued that contract ambiguity was large enough to dominate the forecast.
sections.Best countercase: The strongest countercase, best preserved by variant-view and partly by risk-manager, is that the market is underpricing how easily BTC can move 2k+ in a day and how a single wick or selloff into the exact settlement minute could flip the outcome even if BTC spends most of the period above 72k.
sections.Encapsulated assumptions: Shared assumptions: Binance contract interpretation is standard; BTC around 74.2k is the right current anchor; no extreme venue malfunction occurs. Contested assumptions: whether a ~3% cushion with <24h left deserves something like 89% or closer to 94%. Fragile assumptions: no overnight macro/geopolitical shock and no unusual microstructure distortion near the minute close.
sections.Encapsulated evidence map: Strongest supporting evidence: direct Binance checks across lanes showed BTCUSDT around 74.2k with recent 1-minute closes well above 72k; Polymarket rules clearly define the settle mechanic. Strongest contradictory evidence: BTC can plausibly move >3% in under a day, and the market settles on one exact minute. Governing source-of-truth evidence: Polymarket rules plus Binance API/time/kline interpretation. Mixed evidence: coherent strike ladder suggests an informed market, but that same market may still be mildly overconfident.
sections.Evidence weighting: Most weight went to direct contract wording and direct Binance settlement-venue price checks. Secondary contextual media checks were downweighted because they were not decisive for resolution and added little independence. I effectively ignored broad narrative BTC bull/bear arguments because this case is mostly short-horizon path and mechanics risk.
sections.Counterpoints / strongest disconfirming: The strongest disconfirming evidence is not a contradictory fact but the live mechanism: only a modest BTC drawdown is needed to break Yes, and crypto can do that within this time window. That remains the best reason the market might still be right or even slightly underpricing No.
sections.Resolution or source-of-truth interpreta: Synthesis accepts the lanes' contract read: the decisive observation is the Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute candle corresponding to Apr 16 12:00 ET, using its final Close, with Yes only if that Close is strictly above 72,000. Contract ambiguity is minor rather than none because the exact practical UI/API mapping was inferred and sanity-checked, not observed live at settlement.
sections.Why this could create or destroy alpha: If traders are anchoring too heavily on current spot and too lightly on exact-minute path risk, No may be slightly underpriced. But because the edge versus market is only a few points and the directional case for Yes is strong, any alpha here is likely modest and decays rapidly as settlement approaches if BTC stays elevated.
sections.What would falsify this interpretation /: A fresh pre-settlement Binance check still well above 74k with subdued realized volatility would push the view closer to market. A move into the low 73k or 72k area, a sharp risk-off headline, or new evidence of Binance candle-interpretation friction would push the estimate materially lower.
sections.Highest-value next research: One fresh direct Binance BTC/USDT spot and 1-minute-candle check immediately before and through the Apr 16 12:00 ET minute.
sections.Source-quality assessment: Primary source class was strong: Polymarket rules for contract definition and Binance API/venue data for underlying state. Secondary source class was weaker and mostly contextual. Evidence independence was medium, not high, because decisive evidence concentrates in the contract source and settlement venue. Source-of-truth ambiguity was low to minor. The synthesis is not bottlenecked by missing personas, but it is somewhat bottlenecked by limited independent verification of short-horizon volatility risk.
sections.Verification impact: Yes, synthesis used additional cross-lane verification of the raw findings and supporting source notes rather than relying only on sidecars. That comparison confirmed the sidecars were faithful and revealed no material lane-level inconsistency. Extra external truth-finding was limited: attempted web search failed due to bot-detection, so confidence did not increase beyond the upstream direct-source checks.
sections.Persona contribution map: base-rate — supplied the clean outside-view haircut from current cushion to 89% and emphasized exact-minute fragility. catalyst-hunter — contributed the absence of obvious scheduled bearish catalysts and framed the setup as hold-the-line rather than upside-trigger dependent. market-implied — best articulated why the market is directionally right yet still a bit rich, and provided the strongest board-structure/market-baseline framing. risk-manager — most clearly stated the multi-condition failure mechanics and why a ~3% move is not exotic. variant-view — preserved the strongest below-consensus calibration case at 86% by stressing path dependence and narrow intraday resolution.
sections.Reusable lesson signals: Possible durable lesson: single-minute crypto threshold markets deserve a modest discount versus naive spot-cushion intuition even when direction is obvious. Possible underbuilt driver: none obvious beyond existing reliability/operational-risk framing. Possible source-quality lesson: direct exchange API plus explicit timezone mapping is the right minimum standard. Reusable confidence: medium.
sections.Orchestrator review suggestions: review later for durable lesson: no; review later for driver candidate: no; review later for canon or linkage issue: no; review later for swarm-method issue: yes. Reason: the case reinforces a swarm-method calibration pattern—high-probability narrow-settle markets should trigger explicit skepticism about near-certainty even when lanes largely agree.
sections.Recommended follow-up: Wait for a near-settlement Binance check; otherwise treat this as a small below-market Yes view rather than a major contrarian opportunity.
